diagrams.title: Power Consumption Breakdown in CMOS
diagrams.description: Shows the three components of power consumption in CMOS circuits: dynamic/switching power, static/leakage power, and short-circuit power
diagrams.purpose: Understanding where power goes helps target reduction efforts effectively

diagrams.title: DVFS Power States Diagram
diagrams.description: Illustrates the relationship between performance states (P-states) and their corresponding voltage/frequency/power levels
elements: Transition arrows showing state changes based on workload
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how processors scale down power consumption during lower workload periods

diagrams.title: Processor C-State Power Flow
diagrams.description: Shows processor power consumption at different C-states from active (C0) to deepest sleep (C7)
elements: Wake-up latency increases with deeper states
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how idle power states contribute to overall energy savings

diagrams.title: Power vs Voltage Relationship Graph
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the quadratic relationship between supply voltage and power consumption
elements: Annotation showing: 10% voltage reduction ≈ 19% power savings
Annotation showing: 20% voltage reduction ≈ 36% power savings
diagrams.purpose: Visual proof that voltage reduction is highly effective for power savings

diagrams.title: Dark Silicon Problem Illustration
diagrams.description: Shows a processor chip with highlighted active regions versus dark (powered off) regions due to thermal constraints
elements: Processor die showing multiple core clusters
Active cores (colored) - only ~20-30% can run simultaneously
Dark silicon regions (gray) - powered off due to thermal limits
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the fundamental limitation in modern processor design
